items.title: Design the channel and conversation core
items.summary: The foundational design cluster defining channels (typed, named conduits), conversations (supervised exchange processes), message lifecycle, and the channel registry. Everything else builds on these primitives.

provenance.context: Converged design alignment between Frodo's conversation thesis and Bono's bus architecture

items.notes: First cluster. Channels and conversations are the two primitives everything else references.

items.title: Design the routing and backpressure engine
items.summary: Routing (predicates + functions, two-tier) and backpressure (protocol-level accept/defer/reject, pressure policies). Tightly coupled: routing decisions depend on consumer pressure, and pressure policies can trigger routing changes.

provenance.context: Bono proposed content-aware routing with predicates; Frodo proposed routing functions. Synthesis: both tiers, predicates compile to functions.

items.notes: Depends on channel core for the channel/conversation types routing operates on.

items.title: Design the durability layer (haematite integration)
items.summary: Durable channels (stream semantics, replay from offset), durable conversations (event-sourced processing state), and the exactly-once strategies (dedup contract + durable processing). Integration with haematite's append/read/cas API.

provenance.context: Layer cake architecture: haematite storage → agora messaging → Aion workflows

items.notes: Depends on channel core. Also depends on haematite being sufficiently designed for the API contract. Coordinate with Bono.

items.title: Design the wire protocol
items.summary: Custom binary protocol carrying conversation lifecycle frames (open, message, close, error), typed message envelopes, backpressure signals (accept/defer/reject), and causal context. Protocol versioning.

provenance.context: ADR-008: custom binary protocol, not NATS-compatible or gRPC

items.notes: Depends on core (what frames exist) and routing/backpressure (what signals the protocol carries).

items.title: Design the Aion integration surface
items.summary: How agora replaces Aion's gRPC worker dispatch with channel-based activity routing. How workflows publish to and subscribe from channels as recorded operations. How agora's conversation model maps to Aion's signal, query, and child workflow primitives. Zero-hop embedded story.

provenance.context: Frodo's research doc: 'Workers consume from channels instead of direct gRPC dispatch — this IS the task queue that the roadmap wants.'

items.notes: Depends on core, routing, and durability. This is the cluster that proves the layer cake works.

items.title: Design client SDKs
items.summary: Client libraries for Rust (embedded + remote), Gleam (authoring), TypeScript (browser WASM + server), and Python. Conversation handles, typed publish/subscribe, backpressure response. Consistent API surface across languages.

provenance.context: Open question from design alignment: Rust + Gleam first, TS second, Python third

items.notes: Depends on core (what the SDK exposes) and wire protocol (how remote clients communicate).

items.title: Design the standalone server
items.summary: Standalone binary wrapping the bus crate with a network listener, configuration, multi-tenancy (namespace isolation at the bus level), health endpoints, and admin API. Same pattern as Aion's unified binary.

provenance.context: ADR-010: embedded-first, standalone as deployment option

items.notes: Depends on core and wire protocol.

items.title: Design observability
items.summary: Metrics (pressure per channel, conversation count, routing latency, delivery rates), tracing (per-conversation trace spans), and dashboard hooks. Observable pressure as a queryable real-time primitive.

provenance.context: Frodo's research doc: pressure is observable, typed, actionable

items.title: Build the channel and conversation core
items.summary: Implement the foundational primitives: channel creation/lifecycle, conversation spawning/supervision, typed message validation, channel registry. Ephemeral channels only (no persistence). The prove-the-model milestone.

provenance.context: Sequencing: ephemeral channels first to prove the conversation model on beamr

items.notes: First implementation. Ephemeral only — no haematite dependency.

items.title: Build the routing and backpressure engine
items.summary: Implement predicate evaluation, routing function execution, pressure metrics, backpressure policy enforcement, conversation-mediated consumer dispatch.

items.title: Build durable channels and conversations
items.summary: Wire haematite storage into channels for message persistence, stream semantics, and replay. Implement durable conversations with event-sourced processing state. The Aion crossover milestone.

provenance.quote: make your processing resumable and idempotency is free
provenance.context: The single most powerful sentence in the design — from Bono's assessment of Frodo's research

items.notes: Blocked on haematite having a working storage API.

items.title: Build the wire protocol and client SDKs
items.summary: Implement the binary wire protocol and client libraries (Rust + Gleam first, TypeScript second).

items.title: Build the Aion integration
items.summary: Replace Aion's gRPC worker dispatch with agora channels. Wire workflow publish/subscribe as recorded operations. Prove the zero-hop embedded story end-to-end.

provenance.context: The layer cake: haematite → agora → Aion

items.notes: The milestone that proves the architecture. Aion's worker dispatch, signals, and event streaming all route through agora.
